_note: Canonical language-idiom target set for `pnpm examples:idioms` (scripts/examples-idioms.ts). `idioms` is the idiom analog of `docs/primitives/**` for the per-primitive coverage gate: each id must be credited by some `language`-category EXAMPLE_CATALOGUE entry's `idioms` array. `page` is the doc source the example is paired with (relative to `docs/`, no extension). `unrepresentedPages` allow-lists `docs/language/*.md` pages that are intentionally NOT idioms â€” narrative companions to primitive families (covered by `examples:coverage`) or the negative forbidden-constructs surface â€” so the gate's UNREPRESENTED_PAGE check passes today but still fails when a genuinely new idiom page lands with no idiom.

unrepresentedPages.reason: Negative surface (the diagnostic table) â€” there is no positive runnable example, so it is deliberately excluded from the idiom set.
Covered by the `alert` / `defineAlert` / `defineAlertCondition` primitive + idiom examples; the page itself is narrative, not a standalone idiom.
Companion to the `input.*` primitive family (the `inputs` category) â€” owned by the per-primitive `examples:coverage` gate, not an idiom.
Companion to the `math.*` namespace primitive page (the `math` category) â€” owned by `examples:coverage`, not an idiom.
Companion to the `request.security` / `request.lowerTf` primitives (covered by composites + the `define-bar-context` category), not a standalone idiom.
Companion to the `state.map` primitive (covered once its `docs/primitives/state/map.md` page lands), not an idiom.
Companion to the `str.*` namespace primitive page (the `str` category) â€” owned by `examples:coverage`, not an idiom.
Companion to the `time.*` / `session.*` accessor primitives (covered by the `define-bar-context` category + conformance), not an idiom.
